title: [non-record] AutoEvolve: competition-faithful autonomous search loop for Parameter Golf
author: zanwenfu
body:
## Summary

This PR adds AutoEvolve, a competition-faithful autonomous research loop for Parameter Golf.

AutoEvolve starts from the current local/public SOTA baseline, benchmarks a local incumbent, searches on long-horizon 1xH100 proxy runs, and promotes only validated improvements toward final 8xH100 / 600s evaluation. The goal is not generic hyperparameter sweeping, but a stateful autonomous research process that learns from both wins and failures.

## What is included

- refreshed AutoEvolve baseline to the current top local record (`1.1194`)
- long-horizon `1xH100` proxy mode and competition-fidelity `8xH100` final mode
- incumbent + bounded frontier search instead of naive greedy-only continuation
- structured memory dossier derived from committed experiment state
- repeat-family guard to prevent wasting runs on the same failed proposal family
- rollback and active-base recovery after failed or non-improving runs
- timeout-stage parsing and partial telemetry preservation
- durable logging / result persistence and RunPod monitoring utilities
- dedicated AutoEvolve README and root README pointer

## Validation

- `python3 -m py_compile autoevolve/evolve.py autoevolve/monitor.py`
- `bash -n autoevolve/runpod_launch.sh`
- `python3 autoevolve/monitor.py --summary`
- live dry-run through `gpt-5.4` succeeded; proposal applied cleanly and dry-run left no persistent experiment residue

## Scope

This is a non-record infrastructure/research submission. It does not claim a new leaderboard score yet; it contributes a reproducible autonomous experimentation system intended to generate and validate future non-record and record-quality submissions more efficiently.
